name: Mare of the Mares and Vega de Conde Reservoir — circular trail running from the Fish Lake (GR-84)
name_original: Laguna de las Yeguas y Embalse Vega de Conde — trail running circular desde la laguna de los Peces (GR-84)
region_intro: Jogging around Galende, located in the Sanabria region of Zamora, Spain, offers diverse terrain for runners. The area is characterized by the glacial Lago de Sanabria, Spain's largest glacial lake, and its surrounding natural park. Runners can explore routes through ancient oak, chestnut, and hazelnut forests, as well as along the banks of the River Tera. The landscape includes varied elevations, from flat lakeside paths to ascents towards features like the Cascadas de Sotillo. Best jogging routes around Galende The most popular jogging route is Lake Sanabria and Path of the Monks Loop, an 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of Lago de Sanabria. Another top favourite among local runners is Mare of the Mares and Vega de Conde Reservoir — circular trail running from the Fish Lake (GR-84), a difficult 10.7 miles (17.3 km) path. This route features reservoirs and glacial landscapes. Local runners also love the Lake Sanabria – Viewpoint of Lake Sanabria loop from Monasterio de San Martín de Castañeda, a 12.2 miles (19.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with viewpoints overlooking Lago de Sanabria, often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes. Jogging around Galende is defined by its glacial lake, dense forests, and river paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging ascents to more moderate trails. The routes in Galende are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Galende's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes around Galende?
A: Galende offers a diverse range of terrain for runners. You'll find relatively flat paths along the shores of &lt;strong&gt;Lago de Sanabria&lt;/strong&gt; and the banks of the &lt;strong&gt;River Tera&lt;/strong&gt;. For a more challenging experience, routes ascend through ancient oak, chestnut, and hazelnut forests, leading to features like the &lt;strong&gt;Cascadas de Sotillo&lt;/strong&gt;. Some paths can be rocky or even watery, especially towards the waterfalls, providing a varied workout.

Q: Are there any running routes that feature waterfalls in Galende?
A: Yes, the region is known for its beautiful waterfalls. You can explore routes that lead to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4708534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, traversing through pristine forests. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall – Viewpoint of Lake Sanabria loop from Monasterio de San Martín de Castañeda&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning views and a significant elevation gain.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Galende area?
A: There are nearly 20 dedicated running routes around Galende listed on komoot. These routes offer a variety of distances and difficulty levels, ensuring there's something for every runner, from moderate trails to more challenging ascents.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the running trails in Galende?
A: Most of the running trails in Galende are considered difficult, with 17 out of 19 routes falling into this category. These often involve significant elevation changes and varied terrain. There are also a couple of moderate routes available for those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: Can I find running routes with scenic viewpoints overlooking Lago de Sanabria?
A: Absolutely. Many routes are designed to showcase the breathtaking views of Spain's largest glacial lake. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Lake Sanabria loop from Ribadelago Viejo&lt;/a&gt; offers spectacular vistas as you navigate its challenging terrain.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Galende?
A: Yes, Galende offers several excellent circular running routes. These loops allow you to start and finish in the same location, providing a convenient way to explore the diverse landscapes, from lakeside paths to forest trails and viewpoints.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while running in Galende?
A: Runners in Galende are immersed in a rich natural environment. You'll encounter the pristine waters of &lt;strong&gt;Lago de Sanabria&lt;/strong&gt;, ancient forests of oak, chestnut, birch, and hazelnut trees, and the flowing &lt;strong&gt;River Tera&lt;/strong&gt;. The glacial origins of the landscape also mean unique geological formations and valleys, with highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4708534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Galende?
A: The running routes in Galende are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the stunning natural scenery, the variety of terrain, and the immersive experience of running through ancient forests and along the glacial lake.

Q: Are there any shelters or huts along the running routes in Galende?
A: Yes, for longer runs or in case of unexpected weather, there are several shelters and huts in the wider Galende area. Notable options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3306935" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vega de Conde Shelter&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cárdena Mountain Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Pedro Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, which can provide a resting point during your adventure.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Galende?
A: Galende offers beautiful running conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. While summer is also popular, it can be warmer. Winter running is possible, but some higher-elevation paths might be rocky or even covered in snow, adding to the adventurous spirit.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest to look out for on the running trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4708534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a major highlight. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2643119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín Cave&lt;/a&gt; or various traditional paths connecting local villages, offering glimpses into the region's history and architecture.
tips:
The Laguna de los Peces is a beautiful place that is worth visiting. It gives us some beautiful prints throughout the year, yes, in winter the road can be closed and the accesses on foot frozen.

Mountain lagoon of glacial origin that is located at 1707 meters above sea level in the vicinity of Sanabria Lake, thus belonging to the protected area of the Sanabria Lake and Surroundings Natural Park.

Following the GR 84 long-distance trail, we pass Laguna de las Yeguas, also known as Laguna de Cubillas or Laguna Murias. Located at 1,795 meters and at the foot of Peña Cabrita, it offers a beautiful view. Located within the Sanabria Lake Natural Park and the Segundera and Porto mountain ranges, it is about two kilometers from Lago de los Peces, where we can leave our vehicle.
Located in a spectacular mountain setting, the Rio Pedro refuge is equipped with bunk beds, a fireplace, tables and chairs.
